task11_moved_to_wave5.note: Task 11 is now in Wave 5 (depends on Wave 4). Wave 4 contains Task 10. No wave conflict.

decision_details_removed_from_tasks.note: Tasks 3, 5, 6 now use 'per Decision N' cross-references only. No embedded rationale or alternatives text found in task descriptions.

verify_smoke_task8.note: Task 8 has concrete Verify-smoke: `node -e "const {keyboard} = require('@nut-tree/nut-js'); console.log(typeof keyboard.type)"` confirming nut-js binary loads.

verify_smoke_task4.note: Task 4 has Verify-smoke (OS port allocation check) and Verify-user (browser login flow). Both present.

findings.issue: Decision 5 (PORTABLE_EXECUTABLE_DIR) is missing the 'Alternatives considered' subsection. All other decisions include it; this one ends after Rationale.
findings.fix: Add 'Alternatives considered: app.getPath("exe") rejected — returns temp extraction dir for portable builds, not the actual exe location.' to Decision 5.

findings.issue: Task 2 (Config & Auth Store) has no Verify-smoke. `keytar` is a native Node addon with prebuilt binaries — same binary-load risk as nut-js (covered by Verify-smoke in Task 8). A failed keytar binary would silently break all auth.
findings.fix: Add Verify-smoke: `node -e "const keytar = require('keytar'); console.log(typeof keytar.getPassword)"` → prints `function`.

findings.issue: Task 5 description references 'per Decisions 6–7', but Decision 6 is exclusively about snap-camera-server lens search (which belongs to Task 6). Task 5 is about Channel Points API + EventSub — only Decision 7 (only_manageable_rewards=true) applies.
findings.fix: Change 'per Decisions 6–7' to 'per Decision 7' in Task 5 description.

summary: All four round-1 issues are resolved: Task 11 is correctly placed in Wave 5 with no wave conflicts; decision details are absent from task descriptions in Tasks 3, 5, and 6; Verify-smoke is present and concrete in both Task 8 (nut-js binary) and Task 4 (OS port allocation). Three minor findings remain: Decision 5 missing alternatives, Task 2 missing keytar binary smoke check, and an imprecise Decision cross-reference in Task 5. No critical findings — status is approved.
